cards.title: Core Non-Life Rules
cards.content:
• Strict Application of Indemnity
• Condition of Average (Under-Insurance Rule)
• Subrogation and Salvage
• Proximate Cause (Causa Proxima)

cards.title: 1. Condition of Average (MUST READ)
cards.content:
Applied when Sum Insured < Actual Value. Claim is reduced proportionately. 
Formula: (Sum Insured / Actual Value) * Actual Loss.

cards.title: 2. Salvage Rule
cards.content: After paying full claim, the damaged property (salvage) legally belongs to the Insurance Company.

cards.title: 3. Subrogation & Contribution
cards.content:
Subrogation: Insurer gets right to sue guilty third party after paying claim.
Contribution: Multiple insurers share the loss proportionately. (No double profit).

cards.title: 4. Proximate Cause
cards.content: The active, efficient cause of loss. Example: If fire (insured) causes water damage (by firemen), fire is the proximate cause.

cards.title: Q1. Calculate claim for 50% under-insurance?
cards.content: Answer: Claim will be 50% of the loss (Condition of Average).

cards.title: Q2. Ownership of a wrecked car after claim?
